payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yep, so you have a night's rest after this, so that's-- actually, no. You traveled on a boat for a few days. That would have gone away! That's right, because you had to make a whole journey back to Emon, so you've rested up heavily in the boat. Forget I said anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I kind of remember people going through that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys curl up to get back to sleep, and as you do and the darkness takes you once more, you hear the faint echoing whisper in the back of your head as you fall back into your dreams, and it says, "My fate-touched." Now, the rest of you wake up the next morning. It's been a while since you've been back in Greyskull Keep, and it is strangely heartening to be back at home without another constant threat of death, without the looming of dragon destruction. And it brings back a flood of memories of when you first had this keep made, the innocence of those days and the things you've learned since, and the new appreciations you've gained through the struggles you've had in the process. The other thing catches you is the smell of fresh breakfast being made downstairs, as Laina apparently is already on task.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to talk about it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do a little bit of Druidcraft, something like lightning bugs in the room, and make them dance. Nice and calming, just trying to get back to sleep.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys curl up to get back to sleep, and as you do and the darkness takes you once more, you hear the faint echoing whisper in the back of your head as you fall back into your dreams, and it says, "My fate-touched." Now, the rest of you wake up the next morning. It's been a while since you've been back in Greyskull Keep, and it is strangely heartening to be back at home without another constant threat of death, without the looming of dragon destruction. And it brings back a flood of memories of when you first had this keep made, the innocence of those days and the things you've learned since, and the new appreciations you've gained through the struggles you've had in the process. The other thing catches you is the smell of fresh breakfast being made downstairs, as Laina apparently is already on task.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to talk about it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do a little bit of Druidcraft, something like lightning bugs in the room, and make them dance. Nice and calming, just trying to get back to sleep.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Kendrick is currently watching up on the wall. Nitebe, I haven't seen since you sent him off to Wildmount."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's my memory! 100 gold a month for each of the guards, and then what was the extra?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Five of them get 100 gold a month and two of them get 50 gold a month. I don't remember why or who, but that's how-- I think the guards get 100 and the cooks get 50, or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See, as I wrote down, I believe Shayne, Cordell, Jarett, Kendrick, and Nitebe get 100 a month.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And then we always gave Jarett a bonus because he was captain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. And because he was hot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Well. There you go. Although Jarett is technically not on your payroll anymore. Jarett's now a member of the Pale Guard in Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's right. So, reducing our costs. We're getting better at this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you know where the rest of the crew is, Shayne?

payload.raw_text: "All right. I'll get on it, then. I think I should have some time to go into town and do this here within the afternoon." She grabs another roll off the table and takes a bite of it. "Glad to have you back." And she turns around and leaves the room.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Are you going to audition these new guards like we did you?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Oh, I can figure something out. Don't worry."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I like it. Make sure they're trustworthy, if we're not going to be around. And I hand her 500 gold for herself and for Kendrick.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: And the three that you hire.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes you about three hours, round trip, to go into the city, locate the location, and purchase-- relatively inexpensive, comparatively, about ten gold pieces for materials to do this. Which is expensive for anyone who's just-- and a lot of it is burlap material that's gone over with a weatherproofing liquid, that when it solidifies, it gives almost a rubbery texture. It's used for carts that are traveling a long distance under bad weather. And you figure that might resemble some sort of a skin-type texture at a distance. You gather other elements to build a mask, to wrap things in material, to block things that might look metallic. So yeah, so you gather things that might work in your favor. So as you collect this, what are you guys working on?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you've done a couple of things. But it's not an ability you have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, you do have an ability, but it's only for you, it's not for Doty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You own a cardboard box with a bunch of stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So here's what we do. First and foremost, you have to go to the town and gather supplies because your disguise kit is designed for very small prosthetics and things. This is a large endeavor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. So I'm going to need you to roll an investigation check. This is your ability to go around the town and find, through the midst of chaos of Emon trying to repair itself, somebody who can sell you the supplies necessary.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But a 20? That's still fine. All right, it takes you--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 22. It takes you the better part of the next two hours, but you manage to create some--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you have a kiln that's designed more for-- it's more of a metal-use forge.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's more of a metal-use forge.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So it'll destroy ceramics. It burns at a certain heat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is the best thing we've ever wasted time on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If prototype one fails, I'll make it out of pewter.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. This is an adjacent skill set. You're not going to get your proficiency bonus to it, but you do have materials to essentially try it. I want you to go ahead and roll a base dexterity check. Just add your dexterity modifier. You're not proficient.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm trusting in Gil, because he also can't make a pot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Say the thing, say the incantation!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Mecca lecca high, mecca hiney ho, don't fuck me, Gil.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where are those pottery tools, Gil?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll say the better part of two hours or so to craft them, and then you begin to heat them in the kiln. It'll take you a while for them to harden and set.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm trusting in Gil, because he also can't make a pot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Say the thing, say the incantation!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Mecca lecca high, mecca hiney ho, don't fuck me, Gil.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where are those pottery tools, Gil?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 22. It takes you the better part of the next two hours, but you manage to create some--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for helping me Ghost, by the way. Never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They have to cool and harden, but in the process of crafting it and heating it, you do have some decent ceramic plates that you think might work. They're going to be fragile.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not like they'll crumble on their own walking, but, if they take any sort of chip and damage the ceramic.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hands and face is what I was hoping for.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Ah, it was getting too long, anyway." She makes her way down the ladder and just walks past you into the keep. Comes back about half an hour later, head completely shaved smooth, and she's got, in a little square of fabric, a cluster of all of her red hair. She balls it up, comes over.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, where's Shorthalt when you need him?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no, no, no. And I reach into my bag of holding and pull out 100 gold and say, it would mean a lot to me and to my teammates if you would cooperate in this mission. It might even save a life.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She gives you this long, stern, dark look. "(laughs) Make it 500 and you got a deal."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: In spite of the absurdity of what you've managed to accomplish with the better part of your day, as the sun is now setting, and the rest of you have been wondering what the fuck is going on down there, you manage to build some semblance of a muscular structure underneath the robes. And amongst the porcelain mask that Percy had set as a base, you have crafted texture, color, it looks kind of like a face.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's how nimble your fingers are. It's a dexterity bonus plus the proficiency modifier for a disguise kit. If you are proficient with this kit, with the disguise kit?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't think so. I mean, I don't know, so I'm just going to say no.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just roll a d20, add your dex bonus.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. All right, I'm going to go for like, a General Krieg-type look to the face? I'm going to do my best--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, here we go. I'm going to use Luck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: General Krieg, it's good to see ya!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (laughs) All right, so you guys make your way up to the Platinum Sanctuary, you head to the base of the central mountain that the north end of Vasselheim is built around, to the winding stair that leads up to the Sanctuary proper. There, as you ascend, you can see the sun is getting to the three to four o'clock in the afternoon position. The color of the sky's starting to turn a bit different. You guys make your way to the outside doors of the Sanctuary, where you see the guards are there, stationed, and as you approach, some of them put up a bit of an aggressive front from a defensive standpoint, but one of them does recognise you and approaches, pulls the front of his helmet up. "You are Vox Machina."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: As they leave, I spank Grog hard on the ass and say, money was meant to be spent!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) All right, you scatter into three different directions. First--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, you were going to the Platinum Sanctuary.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Which is to the place where they--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you guys both say goodbye and then you're walking the same direction?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you're parked over here, too? Oh, okay, cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm tired-- okay, this is awkward now, we'll just keep--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They're right next door, they're connected, they're partners.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well some people go this way, and some people go that way and some people go both ways!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. About 15 minutes or so pass as you guys are led up. It's a gorgeous view from up here, by the way, I mean, for Vasselheim, the cloud cover is probably 70 to 80% of the year, with coming storms and sleet. This is one of the few days you've been during this part of the season where the sky is open. You actually see some blue in the sky, and as the light of the day begins to grow more towards a dusk color you start seeing the distant change and it's just endless thick, dense forest of the Vesper Timberland all around the city. And so there's kind of this serene waiting period as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) All righty. Highbearer Vord. He says, "Very well. Please to be patient, I believe he is busy."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And he heads inside, the doors close behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As he does that, I look to Vax and go, hey, I have an idea. So the book we have, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if instead of leaving it here, we left it at the Raven Queen's sanctuary? Don't you think they would keep it more safe? Because she hates undead, right? This is like her nemesis, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She does. Yeah, I mean, that's a whole thing, I don't think that's a bad idea, but I feel like if I go there, I need to stay for a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, cool. Let's forget I mentioned anything.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. About 15 minutes or so pass as you guys are led up. It's a gorgeous view from up here, by the way, I mean, for Vasselheim, the cloud cover is probably 70 to 80% of the year, with coming storms and sleet. This is one of the few days you've been during this part of the season where the sky is open. You actually see some blue in the sky, and as the light of the day begins to grow more towards a dusk color you start seeing the distant change and it's just endless thick, dense forest of the Vesper Timberland all around the city. And so there's kind of this serene waiting period as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) All righty. Highbearer Vord. He says, "Very well. Please to be patient, I believe he is busy."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And he heads inside, the doors close behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As he does that, I look to Vax and go, hey, I have an idea. So the book we have, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if instead of leaving it here, we left it at the Raven Queen's sanctuary? Don't you think they would keep it more safe? Because she hates undead, right? This is like her nemesis, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She does. Yeah, I mean, that's a whole thing, I don't think that's a bad idea, but I feel like if I go there, I need to stay for a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, cool. Let's forget I mentioned anything.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So there you go. You have a tome wrapped in cloth under your arm. You guys spend about an hour or so asking around. The locals, they're-- not saying they're not friendly, but not all of them are well-versed in what you're asking. You say library, some of them are more simpler folk are like, "I don't--" and eventually somebody says, "Oh yeah, you can head over to the-- what's it called? The Cobalt Vault. It's in the southern region of the Quad Roads."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But I would assume if we said, "we're taking the book", that would mean--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you guys said you're taking the book; Grog, you heard them take the book?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I thought you said you were taking the book to go look at the wherever.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. I'll take that as consensus to say that you guys have the book.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I used the book to make the wig. Is that okay? (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys, not having a bag of holding, are clutching the tome-- it's a big tome, it's like--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I thought we put it in a box, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, we didn't have a box.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There was no box. It was a book wrapped in cloth in the bag of holding.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the structure
payload.raw_text: The Cobalt Reserve is the center of the Cobalt Soul monk order, the Ioun worshipers that gather information and maintain it. As Ioun's presence was shattered slightly during the Calamity, in the battle against the chained god, her presence in Vasselheim was diminished and then regrew through the Cobalt Soul in Westruun, so it's expanding back into Vasselheim. You make your way to the structure. The exterior of it is this light-grey stone, it's a cylindrical building with three different doorways around the outside and a giant, bright blue and black marbled dome on top. It looks somewhat transparent, like it's made of a glass-type material. As you step into the front, you can see a central chamber within the inside that's also cylindrical, so you have a small walk-around hallway on the immediate inside of this giant structure, and then the actual library is in the interior, and so on the outside, you see a few books and there are some paintings and tapestries on the walls and you see a few varying monks walking around, a handful that are curious and turn as you walk in. They're all wearing the uniform, dark grey and blue robes of the Cobalt Soul, and there is a desk at the front, where you have a young woman of olive skin sitting, her hair short and wavy with very thick spectacles adorning the front of her nose. Young-ish, maybe mid-twenties. She looks up as soon as you guys approach. "Good day. I fear we're still in the process of preparing the interior, but what do you require?"

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: So there you go. You have a tome wrapped in cloth under your arm. You guys spend about an hour or so asking around. The locals, they're-- not saying they're not friendly, but not all of them are well-versed in what you're asking. You say library, some of them are more simpler folk are like, "I don't--" and eventually somebody says, "Oh yeah, you can head over to the-- what's it called? The Cobalt Vault. It's in the southern region of the Quad Roads."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's exactly what I was looking for, thank you so much.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "All right, yeah, I'll show you." And the guy gives you a couple directions down the road, and you make your way there, and it's similar to the Cobalt Reserve in Westruun, but it's about a quarter of the size.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (laughs) He gives a curious look. "But of course." And he snaps his fingers, and the other monks, he just darts off for a second and then returns a moment later with a small wooden case that opens up, and on the inside there are four different types and thickness of reading glass you can go through. You find one that'll work okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to show you a really weird birthmark, don't panic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, if he really was a demon, why would he need to do research?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Who is my father?! I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It does not specify, but it says you gain the abilities.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think I've read it says you keep the same intellect, though, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you keep the same intellect.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You keep the same mental stats.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you retain all your skills and saving throw and proficiencies, in addition to gaining those of the creature.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to take a moment also before this happens, open the Common book, take a moment, and gently ask the good sir if they have a reading glass around, because I still don't have any reading glasses.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: There is very little talk about celestial blood, divas, other than the fact that there's been a long-standing battle since the founding. Since the prime gods and betrayer gods divided early in the creation of Exandria and--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and the pit fiend you killed probably returned and got demoted.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, to a lesser? Like they get turned into something smaller?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If something really powerful and cool that was given a station was suddenly walking through the street and killed by a bunch of people in the city and came back and was like, "they killed me," they'd be like, "you're weak obviously". Reading the structure, you probably really ruined that guy's day. (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He's probably going to have centuries getting his shit back together. He's probably really pissed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you basically made him lose his house, and his dog, and his wife. You turned him into a country song.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do they talk about anything like the children that we saved? The effect of angelic, angelic, why those little angel children--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I can give you the rest of this to read on the break, if you'd like because it's a lot of information, but that's the one that sticks out to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's one of the safer areas for a mortal to walk through, comparatively-- it's still in one of the Hells. But because it is such a center for business, it's a popular location for extra-planar travelers, it's a popular place for succubi, incubi to travel, it's a popular place for rakshasas. It specifically brings up hags, creatures that deal in business, deal in exchanges, deal in contracts. Beyond the usual devil ones, a lot of them tend to go to the Iron City of Dis.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm marking that-- I'm putting a pin on my Pinterest board.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Atlas Obscura the hell out of the City of Dis.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: About five minutes of walking before two armored Bastions come up in their piecemeal leather and scale breastplate armor with their heavy fur cloaks over their shoulders, holding spears. "You!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: While you guys are completing your research-- Taryon and Grog! What are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have the book? (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Never mind. Let's go find a place to buy some potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right. Are you accustomed to buying things?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Do you have a personal shopper?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nice. Well, show me how it's done.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'll take you under my wing, so to speak.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm a bit big so I don't know how you're going to do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And as you guys walk through the streets, everyone stares. Partially because you are a bright shiny beacon of finely polished gems and armor flanked by a massive bearded goliath and whatever the fuck Doty is.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hello, hi, greetings, good day, good afternoon, don't mind them, they're my friends! Hahahaha!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You're getting close to the exterior of the building pointed to by the guards, and you recognize it. It's the place where you've previously purchased potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They don't care what people think.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (loudly) Ah, just want to take a load off and just rest here for a while. Not going anywhere, just sitting here and acting real boring, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep. How are we going to stay here and find the health potions at the same time?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Guide me. You've been doing this longer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, you're really doing well. I'm learning a lot right now. Clearly, you are a well-traveled man of mystery.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I've read about situations like this, right? We just need to create a diversion. While the guards are diverted, we run away, fast as we can, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I literally told you about that one minute ago.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "No, I'm waiting on-- Trade's been a little slow. Looks like things are getting up in Emon, so hopefully we'll have more soon. But for the time being, I do have some more healing potions." And he goes into the back room, and you hear him rummaging for a minute. You hear one crate fall over and a couple of things shatter. "Ah god damn--". And eventually he comes back through the door, and he's holding in his arms a few clusters of various glass vials. Some of them have been repurposed and taped at the top, but they're functional. He sets them down on the counter. "All right. I have six potions of healing, and I have three of greater healing, and one superior."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks you over. "The scroll I would put at a fair 6,000 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (gulps) Watch how it's done. I'm going to put an amount of gold on the table. I'd like you to consider it, and if it's to your liking, take it, and if not, just shake your head. And I'll put 200 gold on the table. I'll put 200 more. I'll put 2,000 more. And another 2,000.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You now have a scroll of fire shield.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Scroll of fire shield, excellent. I don't know what that is.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wow. Do you have anything that makes you faster?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I said I have something that makes you jump."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm pretty good at jumping. What about regular healing potions?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That I've got! You cleared me out of my other various vials last time."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "How would you say to the fair price of 10,000 gold pieces for the batch?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But they use potions. I've seen them use potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm normally pouring them in their mouths.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you think they would appreciate these?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, I hadn't thought that far. As a (mumbling) new member of Vox Machina--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Bad allergies here. It could be like a really great gift, you know? Like Keyleth gave you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "For an additional charge, yes, of course, I gift wrap!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: -- of course for an additional charge. What if we took the whole lot of those for 6,000 gold?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The whole lot? For 6,000. I'm afraid that is far under the price I'd be considering. Let me see here." And he does some kind of notations, and he goes, "Hm. You seem, from our previous experience, to be quite the bargainer, my friend."

payload.raw_text: "Terribly sorry. My apologies. I'll be right to it." And he turns and leaves the room.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "6,000 and the egg, you say? Very well." He pushes the--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "I will be." He goes over and quickly reaches over and grabs a thick towel-like pile of cloth, and wraps it up carefully. "Ooh." Sets it down. "A pleasure doing business with you all."

payload.raw_text: Doty turns. "Ahh!" And comes running after you guys as you exit the shop.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: We should go. We should leave.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right. Thank you for your--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Good day to you. Let's go. Doty, come.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You manage to find one location, but it's been closed for half an hour when you get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Dis City. Not this city, but the City of Dis.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I have that book please?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yes! Hi. Yes, this is-- Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just going to hold on to that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Are you going to hold on-- Do we have a bag to put that in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have Grog's bag of holding, the same one we've always had. Hey, is there somewhere we could buy a fucking bag of holding?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you could ask around and try to find somebody. It'll take a little time. You guys are right now hitting the cusp of dawn. Sorry, dusk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is there somewhere I could find before things close? Just to see?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "All right. That-- Okay." The gentleman seems a bit overwhelmed, but quickly goes ahead and helps you guys find, over the next 30 or so minutes, some very nice dresses. Better than anything you've previously worn. And he gets you something that he thinks royalty should wear. As there is no real royalty in Vasselheim, it is a church-run state. It is more of a frumpy robe with a fur collar.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Get it, girl. Get it, get it, girl.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do manage to find one individual who is in the process of closing their formal attire shop.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I toss them 20 gold. An extra 20 minutes, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What's good for your Ashari people? Something covered in grass, maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, it's a little esoteric for me. A little experimental. A little Gaga-esque.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lots of pink bows, maybe, for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, it reminds a little of when we dressed Trinket up in pink bows.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right, and we could be like--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if that's a good thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to become royalty. Kind of, but not really.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's my first time getting to dress up as royalty.

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the exterior
payload.raw_text: All right. So you guys walk over to the Dusk Meadow. You're met by the same gray, slightly bleak atmosphere on this side of Vasselheim. But you make your way to the exterior of the large structure. The ominous obsidian and gold building that represents the center, if you will, the beating heart of the Raven Queen's worship. As you approach the front stairway, both of you familiar with, one brought in, the other not met with any living person for the most part in your journey, you both approach-- still arms hooked?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "All right, I'll get it taken care of."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right. While that's happening, you two are heading to the Raven's Crest?

payload.raw_text: All righty. As you guys rest for the evening, the morning comes around. You're across the board, fairly hung-over, but functional. Except for Grog, Grog, you're fine. Yay constitution 20! But you guys muster yourselves, get a quick bite to eat downstairs, in the now even-more-empty tavern. Your dresses are at the ready, you go and collect them. What's next, what do you guys want to do?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: You buy the potions, we pay for the shitty hotel rooms.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: (laughs) I don't know if that evens out. But all right.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Don't think of it as a thing that evens out, think of it as an ever-increasing debt that doesn't really ever get paid.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I like the way you think, science bro.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys are walking closer to the outside of Zephra, you see a couple of individuals beginning to approach, three or four children, just seeing these strange visitors in the outskirts. They're also keeping low and not trying to draw your attention too strongly, though they are out in the middle of the open. You see a few bushes and elements. You see two grown individuals walking up with the children, one of which appears to be a relatively young girl, maybe around 13, 14 years old who pushes the kids to the back and goes, "Keyleth?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I forgot you guys haven't seen my hometown yet. Okay, all right. Let's go that way. Okay. See, over there is where I caught my first baby griffin--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: -- he was lost and got separated from his mom, and I captured him up, and I tucked him up under my shirt. And over there actually--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: -- I tripped and fell and that was the first time that I scraped my knee--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: -- and I was like, oh no, I'm bleeding.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: While she was walking, I go behind and do this to Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it was so terrible, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No it's fine, I feel good. I feel really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I feel really good. I feel rested. I feel fucking healthy man. I don't know. I'm serious.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A short time passes. A few moments, before you begin to see the city suddenly come to more vibrant life. People are stepping out of their abodes. Dozens of individuals are beginning to come together, talking, whispering, and some looking back and watching you all in the outskirts of Zephra, until eventually it seems a procession of some kind is all gathering and moving out of this mass of people in your direction. As you look, you begin to see people adorned in similar clothing as to when you first encountered Keyleth in the swamps outside of Stillben. You see these leather-based, almost scaled-looking armor that drifts down to the legs. You see people that hold giant staffs that hold golds and greens and blue colors that mark the symbols of the Zephra tribe. And then, in the center of them, you've met once before, you see stepping forth, Korren, the father of Keyleth and the current acting Voice of Tempest of the Zephra. His long white, silver hair drifting behind him. He approaches with a look. Everyone holds back as he steps forth from the crowd and ignoring the rest of you, just goes and beelines straight to Keyleth. You can see him, he has a bit of a limp to him that you don't recall from last time, but he seems other than that in good health. As he approaches you, he clasps his hands out in front of him and takes a good long look at you as he reaches out and takes your hands into his own. "My daughter."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do you want me to get him?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "C'mon, c'mon kids." And takes them by the hand and they all drag the kids off as you guys wait in the outskirts of the city. One of the kids who didn't quite get a hand on this young three-year-old boy who's running behind them stops. Turns and stares at you all, but stares specifically at Grog and is just wide-eyed, going--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Eat your vegetables, you'll look like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just immediately bolts past to catch up with the rest of the crowd.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So I believe you just set up a lifetime of unreasonable expectations.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've never seen Grog eat a single vegetable--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: -- the entire time I've known him.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, while we still have daylight, let us prepare for the ceremony. Come, come, all of you. You are welcome, welcome here in Zephra. We'll begin, decoration shall commence. Rest, prepare, naught but two or three hours until the ceremony is to occur." He turns, and folks begin to rush in, and people just begin to barrage you with questions and congratulations, and a number of individuals who seem to have heard stories, many second-hand, and exaggerations of the various things that you've accomplished over the past year or so here in Tal'Dorei. You answer questions, you play into the tall tales if you want. One child turns to you and goes, "And who are you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we have defeated the Chroma Conclave. The help of Vox Machina and many many others, at the cost of many lives.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good, good. I'm glad we could be of some aid to your journey."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, thank you. The Air Ashari were very valuable during that day. Very courageous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And we will remember those we lost in that fray. The great sacrifices that they gave so that these children could live. So?" And at this point, he now takes notice of the rest of the group. "By what joy do we have of bringing your allies here and returning to your people?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, I thought Uvenda might have said something. I'm here because I have completed my Aramente, and have come for my blessing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I just wanted to hear it from your lips."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Still waiting on your final word, I guess.

payload.raw_text: Immediately, over the next hour or two, people begin to gather floral wreaths and strips of silk and material that have been prepared specifically for these types of occasions. Many of them new, made from the recent generations in preparation, many that are held over by tradition and by the various leaders that came before you. Many that may have had the hope of the return of Vilya, but instead, carry through with memory of Keyleth. As this transpires, you guys are brought food, you are gathered into a central open celebration. Almost like a pit that descends about two feet, and there are a number of stools and tables, and food and drink are brought about. As opposed to an event and then a celebration afterward, it seems to be a reverse here at Zephra. It's a build-up. It's everyone, the excitement grows. As this is happening, you glance off and there is one very large, singular tree right across the way, at the very edge of the precipice of this rocky outcropping that holds most of the city of Zephra. And there at the edge you see one large, twisting, gnarled tree that curves upward. Not quite Sun Tree in height and size, but still very elegant and beautiful with long, thin branches that gently sway on the outskirts of whatever barrier seems to bend and twist the winds from coming and scattering the village. You watch as people step up to it, and they're leaving jewelry, they're putting large ribbons across it. Elements of it remind you of the tree that was in the center of the temple in Vesrah, though a different texture. It seems to be a deep, vibrant, healthy dark brown, and with elements of gray bark, with moss that grow at the base and tuck under the branches, where the shadows tend to linger throughout the day. Eventually, it seems that they have returned and the decor is set, benches have been moved, and most of the people of Zephra are now shifting in the direction of this tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is a massive interest around Grog as goliaths generally do not travel this far up into the territory of the mountain. Most of them stay far north in the Cliffkeep or wandering the dividing plain. They're just swarming you and climbing up on you and hanging from your arms and you feel kind of awkward but kind of enjoying the attention at the same time.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And they can only gain the temporary hit points from this until they finish a short or long rest. So every short rest you can give everyone 17 temporary hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And with that, you gain the Inspiring Leader feat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What? I get the what now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Inspiring Leader feat, as the end of your Aramente.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit, Voice of the Tempest!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which is pretty fun. What that allows you to do is--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you can totally die. You can spend ten minutes inspiring your companions, shoring up their resolve to fight. When you do so, choose up to six friendly creatures, which can include yourself, within 30 feet that can hear and understand you. Each creature can gain temporary hit points equal to your level plus your charisma modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what! Well, you guys are out of luck with the charisma modifier shit, but what! (all laugh)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Essentially, you take ten minutes to talk everybody up. So start practicing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit, Voice of the Tempest!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which is pretty fun. What that allows you to do is--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you can totally die. You can spend ten minutes inspiring your companions, shoring up their resolve to fight. When you do so, choose up to six friendly creatures, which can include yourself, within 30 feet that can hear and understand you. Each creature can gain temporary hit points equal to your level plus your charisma modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what! Well, you guys are out of luck with the charisma modifier shit, but what! (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And they can only gain the temporary hit points from this until they finish a short or long rest. So every short rest you can give everyone 17 temporary hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You have to give a speech every time.
